gates.name: only_you
gates.description: an observation only this person could make (not a generic take)

gates.name: real_number_or_specific
gates.description: a real number, or a concrete checkable detail

gates.name: concrete_scene
gates.description: a real moment, shown not summarized

gates.name: non_obvious_lesson
gates.description: a takeaway most people would miss

gates.name: no_slop
gates.description: none of the banned slop phrases appear

gates.name: central_claim_human
gates.description: the core claim is the human's, not the model's

dimensions.name: story_strength
dimensions.description: is there a real story with a beginning and a turn

dimensions.name: opinion_edge
dimensions.description: a clear point of view someone could disagree with

dimensions.name: specificity_surprise
dimensions.description: specific and a little surprising, not predictable

dimensions.name: ownability
dimensions.description: only this person could have written it

dimensions.name: voice_match
dimensions.description: matches the voice profile (diction, cadence, tics, never-do list)

dimensions.name: format_adherence
dimensions.description: hook in first line, one idea, short lines, real close, no bait

dimensions.name: audience_fit
dimensions.description: fits the audience's values without losing the voice

dimensions.name: stakes_turn
dimensions.description: a real cost, risk, admission, or contrarian turn — not just pleasant and safe

example_reasons.note: Illustrative only — the tone + specificity bar for the one-line reasons. Match how tough and concrete these are; your real gates/dimensions are listed above. Edit freely.

gate.name: concrete_scene
gate.reason: opens on a thesis ('AI is changing PM'), not a moment you can picture

dimension.name: voice_match
dimension.reason: plain and dry like their samples, but the close drifts warmer than they'd ever write

example_revision.note: Illustrative only — the JSON shape + the kind of critique to write. Match how the critique names ONE concrete fix; never copy the content. Edit freely.
example_revision.critique: Hook lands, but the post stays positive start to finish — there's no turn. Use the admission you already have (the first version broke) so it earns the lesson instead of just stating it.
example_revision.revised_draft: (the rewritten post — same claim, same voice, with the turn added and two filler lines cut)
example_revision.reason: added the turn and tightened; one more pass could sharpen the close
